reviews.title: Very nice speaker
reviews.text: This is a nice speaker. However, it's not as loud as I expected. I did research looking for really loud Bluetooth speakers. Supposedly this was the 2nd loudest speaker in the world behind the Soundbox (which is over $1000). I obviously went with this one. Very easy to use/set up and it is a nice speaker. The sound isn't low, however it definitely isn't something that you'd use at a party, get together, or if you just like really loud music, like I do. I did end up returning this speaker and purchased the Nutex Professional Active Speaker, which is much louder. If you're looking for a speaker thats louder than the smaller Bluetooth speakers, and does fill the room that you're in with sound. Then I would definitely recommend this speaker. If you're wanting a really loud speaker, that you can hear throughout your home, that'll work at a social gathering, etc... then you'll probably want to keep looking.

reviews.title: execellent and two are even better
reviews.text: the sales person a lady made this happen she will be a manager , she has pa-zasss
reviews.author: Termite

reviews.title: You literally cannot go wrong with this. BUY IT
reviews.text:
Ive had this guy for over a year now. This has been through so many different situations and always comes up as a winner.

BATTERY:
The normal battery is perfectly fine for most, however if you want to crank it loud for long, get the bigger battery. That is what I went for.  Its a snap (literally) to install and Ive played it at FULL volume with hip hop/dubstep for 11 hours. Just insane battery life. at 60% it will last around 48 hours of continuous play. It charges relatively slow but thats because i have the 100 W/hr battery, which is expected. In sleep mode, it goes into a REALLY low power use state, and ive left on for a week, and only lost one bar of battery. halve everything ive said for the regular battery.


SOUND: 
Crank this up outside and youll hear it clearly for a quarter of a mile. They are NOT joking when they say this has serious output. Quality is good too, its similar in audio profile to my Sonos play 5 (gen2). The super deep bass is a bit lacking. However, the EQ settings will quickly fix that. Its got a neutral-warm midtone, and a clear, defined high end. Even using the EQ to crank the sub didnt get any distortion at all. This speaker loves to be played loud for a long time. its the PERFECT beach/ pool party speaker (just dont get it wet). Even though theres a ported sub, it makes no unwanted noises like port chuffing or popping. 

EASE OF USE: 
Almost as easy as it can get. paring process is painless (like it should be) connection is stable, and over long distances. the EQ settings and "menu" are easy to use. Only downside is every time you make one adjustment to the EQ, the music pauses for a very short time. thats my only nit pick. Super useful to plug your phone into the USB on the back for charging. 

The sleep mode is super useful too. Just touch one of the lights on top and bam, its awake. 

SIZE: 
Its not a small speaker, thats for sure. But if you take this to any party, youre now the life of that party. its bulky, but for good reason, all that space improves the bass response. Its also got chonky rubber feet on the bottom that really keep it from moving.
reviews.author: ThatAudioDude

reviews.title: Almost perfect, but excellent boom box
reviews.text: This Bluetooth speaker/boom box has incredible bass and volume. It will disturb the neighbors...it’s that loud. Excellent highs and lows and midrange. Soundwise it’s flat out perfect and has long battery life. My only issue is that sometimes it loses Bluetooth connectivity. And it seems to only be an issue with Apple Music. Other apps such as YouTube music play just fine. The menu controls could be a bit better but that’s a minor gripe. You won’t be changing that much

reviews.title: Hands Down best bluetooth speaker
reviews.text:
The speaker takes it to the MAX, the stock battery last about 9 hours at almost full, to full volume, and its FREAKIN LOUD, and THE BASS HITS, AND TRAVELS. It has a 5 channel fully adjustable equalizer. NFC, BLUETOOTH, and AUXILARY INPUT. The REMOTE CONTROL controls everything, from VOLUME, to TRACK, to EQ. I've never heard a better sounding, with such great highs, and super rich, deep bass, bluetooth speaker. It's not small, Nor is its sound, put the back about 3 inches from a WALL or HARD SURFACE, or better yet, IN A CORNER, and the bass port with accentuate your bass even more, reflecting against the surface. I was gonna get the extended battery for 18 hour battery life, but I just dont need it. The carrying case is awesome, and also works pretty good as a bass reflex surface, like a wall. It has pockets for your remote, charging cable, aux in cable, and your digital (or anolog) audio player, (but in the analog 
case, the remote will not control the track). I dont think I've ever been happier with a bluetooth speaker, and I have plenty. You can even plug a microphone into the aux input, or your electric guitar, etc, with a ¼" to ⅛" adapter.

reviews.title: Best portable bluetooth speaker on the planet.
reviews.text:
I have had many bluetooth speakers and this is by far the best. I 
work in a factory where max volume has always been needed even to hear the speaker.  I keep this speaker at half volume and it is loud and easy to hear.  If a great song comes on I may boost it to 3/4 volume and it rocks.  The bass is amazing and having 5 drivers is a game changer.  Now for the best part.  The battery life at half volume and a few boosts to 3/4 volume lasts for a 12 hour day with more to spare.  This is the one to buy.  Every day use for about 2 months.
reviews.author: freefunsite

reviews.title: Just buy it. You won't be sorry.
reviews.text:
I bought this for my son who's somewhat of an audiophile, and he's over the moon. Not only is the speaker easy to move around or take with you, but it sounds great while doing it. 

I've got a Mu-so and a Mu-so QB and the Exos-9 sounds just as good (for some things maybe even better?) The only reason I wouldn't put the Exos-9 in my living room is that it does sort of look like what it is - a portable speaker.  That said, I'm thinking about getting another one to link with the first for actual stereo sound. 

Besides the great sound, the speaker is easy to use. I bought the remote control and it works well, but the young folks apparently eschew remotes in favor of their phones. I don't think the remote will get much use. 

The speaker doesn't feel that heavy to me which may be in part because the grip in the back is quite comfortable to hold onto. I did buy the Aiwa carrying case elsewhere and it's not elegant, but thoughtfully designed and makes it easier to take the speaker on the go. From unboxing to listening took less than five minutes.

reviews.author: Bigpoppa2236

reviews.title: Great sounding bluetooth speaker
reviews.text:
I purchased the EXOS-9 over others in this price class because it is the only one that does not use "passive radiators", and it has an EQ you can tune. All of the speakers are actually speakers. There are a total of five. One subwoofer, two mid-bass drivers and two tweeters. The subwoofer has its own separate amplifier. It's a difference you can hear - and feel. On some music, I actually needed to use the EQ to turn the bass down a little. The mid-range response is clear and the highs are crisp. Is it "Hi-Fi"? No, but remember that we're talking about a portable Bluetooth speaker that uses digital sound processing to make up for the deficiencies in the small speakers. In some program material, you can hear the DSP chip compressing and limiting levels to prevent distortion.Bluetooth is also far from high fidelity, but this unit does have Qualcomm's AptX Bluetooth chip which is capable of much higher quality sound than regular Bluetooth audio. (You'll need a phone that supports AptX audio) Most people won't even notice, but I am a more critical listener than most. For best fidelity, ditch wireless altogether and use the 3.5mm cable input.

 In my 14x35 family room, it shakes the walls with plenty of well-balanced sound. Outside, it provides ample volume. More than enough to annoy your neighbors to the point of calling the cops. When cranked up to near maximum, the battery life is about 6 hours. At reasonable listening levels, it'll go 20 hours no problem. I like the fact you can remove the battery pack and swap out a fresh one. What will you do with your JBL or Sony when the battery wears out in a few years? Their batteries are sealed inside and not user replaceable.

This is what I would call an "adult" Bluetooth speaker. There is no fancy light show and it isn't splash resistant. It looks more at home in an oak bookshelf than sitting on a tailgate. But it will happily do tailgate duty and do a good job too.
reviews.author: ITGuy

I have had big stereos in my time house and auto and this doesn’t compare. I was just expecting more I guess. So I returned it If your looking for a speaker for your garage while your working I recommend it. If you’re looking for a speaker that really pounds I would not recommend it

I don’t think many of people that rate this highly don’t know sound. Honestly it’s not even that loud. I’ve set the equalizer to all positions and tried in Bluetooth and using the cord on my iPhone with iTunes. My phone turned all the way up and the speaker all the way up and it’s not great to me. I played all types of music still not great. No crisp highs and bass is disappointing. I will say it does not distort. I’ve had many stereos in my time and this don’t cut it. Not worth $300 I was at least hoping this thing sounded as good as the older mini shelf stereos and it don’t. I will be returning this. And I’m no longer interested in Bluetooth speakers. They are a joke.

reviews.title: Excellent Speaker
reviews.text: I love this speaker, absolutely love it. It’s a bit pricey but you get what you pay for. It’s not extremely portable so keep that in mind. Its easy enough to move around the house but you’re We also have a Sonos system at home but it’s a pain in the neck. Our internet doesn’t work that great and the Sonos app constantly needs updated. Consequently I use this speaker 99% of the time. No crazy apps, you can adjust the equalizer on the unit and not through an app which is great. If this thing had an FM tuner it would be perfect but for some reason hardly any Bluetooth speaker does anymore. Oh well I love this unit and would highly recommend. It has a nice full sound and doesn’t lose clarity as you crank it up. Bass is great too!

Returned unit, since I loved the sound better than any other speakers I requested for replacement at Bestbuy. I will revisit my review if the replacement unit works good.
reviews.author: Shiva

reviews.title: BIG Lifeless Class D Noise-box
reviews.text:
I hate to bash the Aiwa Exos-9 since so many people seem to love it like it's their baby but it did not live up my expectations or the hype. I would only consider it a value of 5-star for $100 and 4-star for $150 BECAUSE of what it is - portable and powerful. It lacks high-end sound quality which drops it out of an A-League audio purist' speaker. Simply put, it is not pleasing enough to cost $300. I would try the Harman Kardan Go Play for $180 as a cheaper and possibly superior rival. I cannot recommend anything for sure in place of the Aiwa because I have no real life experience with anything in this class but from my research I would consider the Harman Kardan go play ($180), the Fluance fi70 ($500) and possibly the DiamondBoxx ($500-$1700) as the nearest competitors.
 
For most casual listeners I think they will be pleased enough to enjoy the E9. That is not meant to be a put down but some people don't have elevated hearing like I do (my mom and one of my brothers are examples). 
However, audio enthusiast / purist will probably recognize the E9 as a ho-hum (bland, lifeless, run of the mill) speaker with some added bass for bass-heads. It is OK for a hard thumping speaker in a box but it isn't even as loud as I expected it to be. 

The music never made me forget I was listening to it out of a box because it has that box-congestion sound - like a nasally voice, this speaker has a box sound that it couldn't hide.This was the deal breaker for me. 

Build quality seemed sturdy and like care was given. The BT connectivity was fast as a snap. The unit is heavy and pretty large, thus I would call it a semi-portable speaker: You can definitely manage to take it with you but it will be a little bit of trouble doing so.

The built-in equalizer and touchscreen are awkward amenities. Each time you adjust the EQ up or down the sound cut out then went back up to the set volume. I found this irritating. 
The touchscreen isn't very user-friendly or responsive, either. In fact, I had to rub my hand across the top back and forth before it would ever recognize I was trying to use the buttons. I never could quite figure out a fail-proof method to get the touchpad to light-up and respond, I had to keep tapping and rubbing it until it would finally wake-up. Not a deal breaker for me but a nuisance like the EQ.

In conclusion, no amount of adjusting frequency bands is going to be enough for a speaker with the limitations that the Exos-9 has. Standing bass waves, the boxy sound signature, cheap Class D amplification and lifeless mid/highs held me back from keeping the E9 or considering it a deal at $250-$300. I am happy to have back the space it took up and my hard earned $$.

Not happy with the shipping process with Best Buy. Nonetheless, the speaker is awesome. Be sure to purchase this way in advance if you want to have it for an event or look elsewhere. I did a lot of research before purchasing this product and I am glad I did. I was leaning toward to Ion Road Warrior for the tailgate but went with this instead. This sounds much better. This has much deeper bass and midrange. You can also adjust the equalizer to fir your style. You can also link another Exos 9 with it to make it even better. Happy with this product.

reviews.text: I currently have a Altec Lansing IMT800 and love that unit. Two side firing subs, two woofers and two tweeters. I had see all the positive reviews for the Aiwa Exos 9 and ordered it along with the carrying case and remote. After comparing it to my old Altec IMT800 I decided to return it. The Aiwa sounds muffled compared to the Altec. I even removed the metal grill to see if that would help. I have now ordered the Diamondboxx. I am keeping my fingers crossed. I still love my old Altec IMT800 and wish they still made it. Mine is just beginning to have power supply issues due to its age. Altec needs to reintroduce the IMT800/810. I am still rocking out with the old Altec until my Diamondboxx arrives.

Today I received the aiwa and as soon as I turn it on and connected super easy to Bluetooth I was in awe! reviews.title: Proper Performance
reviews.text: I have 2 Aiwa - Exos-9 Portable Bluetooth Speakers that i link together in left / right stereo mode with my ipod / bluetooth. I place them on speaker stands for a day at the park or in the backyard, lift the stands up to a little above ear level while sitting. Both speaker equalizers are set the same (left to right - channel 1 @ +3db / channel 2 @ +2db / channel 3 @ +3db / channel 4 @ +3db / channel 5 @ +3db). The sound that is produced reminds me of my DJ set up using Yamaha/Nexo speakers, just not as powerful, clean and clear, and the sound carries a pretty good distance.

I’ve always had to carry an external power supply with my other portables. Not anymore, these batteries are great and I bought 2 extended life batteries for them. The Audiopipe ATVP 3200 will still be used in wet conditions. But when the audio quality really matters most, it’ll be the Aiwa Exos-9 x2!

reviews.title: Unbelievable price! Product!
reviews.text: I read several reviews, owned several other brands. I checked out some online reviews, read several reviews. Tested every display models I could find. Yes! I was the guy blaring loud music in Best Buy and every other store I could find an Audi device to test. So we took a chance! It's the best money I've spent on a portable, Bluetooth speaker, the sound quality is miles beyond my expectations. The battery life is great. Sounds great, and has big bass. It's definitely loud enough to get the neighbors attention! I can't wait to pair two together
reviews.author: AudioNut432

reviews.title: Limited Functionalities - A Lot of Bugs
reviews.text: This unit has a big problem and that is when connected via Bluetooth to my laptop after a while starts stuttering and I have been going back and forth with Awia about this and they don't have an idea what is wrong. I even replaced the unit with another one thinking being an issue with a faulty unit but the problem exhibited itself again and stuttering painfully when playing music or video from facebook, youtube and other websites. Is anyone else experiencing the same problem?
reviews.author: Tico

reviews.title: Best Bluetooth speaker
reviews.text: I can't ask for more, I'll definitely buy a 2nd one to pair.
reviews.author: Zenglen

reviews.title: Great for parties
reviews.text: Love this speaker! My fiancé and I take it every where we go, to the park, to a friends house or just the back yard. Great sound great look. You have ful control of your eq which is awesome. Easy to connect using Bluetooth or nfc.
reviews.author: Charlybearz

reviews.title: Clear, crisp and loud!
reviews.text: I love Bluetooth speakers they are portable and each speaker has its own purpose. I have my small Bose soundlink mini. I have a mid size beats by dre XL and now this bad boy each one of my speakers was strategically thought out for its purpose and sound quality. aiwa exos 9 sound is phenomenal sounding. yeah you can buy a louder speaker but louder is not always better clarity is king!
reviews.author: Gearhead

reviews.title: Amazing!
reviews.text:
This speaker system is simply amazing. The sound quality is very crisp and clear. This is not your standard "portable" speaker. This is a true stereo sound system that has it's own rechargeable power source. This truly cannot be compared to the Bose portable speakers. This thing is massive in comparison... And the sound quality is well worth the space. 
I bought one to have as a primary countertop stereo that I could also drag outside for parties. I was so happy with my purchase that I bought a second, to pair with the first.
 I've been simply blown away with the sound quality of this system. The bass is true without being obtrusive and the highs are crystal clear. Keep in mind, the sound is uni-directional on this speaker, so placement might be an issue for some users, depending on where you hope to put it. It's not really shelf or countertop friendly, but it does have a modern clean look that doesn't need to be hidden. 
 This system does not come with a remote, but I highly suggest that if you use the system on more than rare occasion, you purchase the remote that Aiwa makes specifically for the Exos-9.
 If you're considering this speaker, and have the space for the larger (roughly 18x20 inches) Exos-9, buy it!
reviews.author: TJinMI

reviews.title: Great speaker
reviews.text: I have played this for about 20 hours and it is as good or better than advertised. My family and friends are just amazed at the sound that pours out of this speaker at low volume or wide open. This is exactly what I was looking for in a Bluetooth speaker and will buy one more when I can. :)
reviews.author: xman

reviews.title: Great sound and great quality
reviews.text: Sounded so good I bought a second one. They sound like your in a club.
reviews.author: Ezekiel

reviews.title: MUST BUY!
reviews.text:
I own a small CrossFit Box which the workout space is about 1000 Square Feet. There is a lot of noise going on there, and I was using an ION Bluetooth speaker, but the music was always drowned out and would get real murky if the volume was too loud. 

The Exos-9 was used last night for the first time and it is amazing! It filled the room with clear, crisp sound and some really heavy bass! No matter how loud i turned it up, the sound didn't distort. This is definitely designed to be more of a replacement for your typical home stereo system.

All I can say is this product delivers.
reviews.author: RedLotusJoe

reviews.title: Excellent Speaker!!!!
reviews.text: The best sound for the size of this speaker. The bass is awesome. I am one very customer. Well done AIWA!!!!

reviews.title: Aiwa Exos-9 Bluetooth speaker = A+ sound
reviews.text: I am really impressed with the Aiwa Exos-9 Bluetooth speaker! The sound is clear and has incredible bass for a portable Bluetooth speaker! The Bluetooth connected within a few second to my Android phone. I listened to it for at least 5 hours on battery power and the power meter only moved one bar! But keep in mind, I did not listen to it full blast the whole 5 hours! I will probably end up buying another one so I can pair them up and have double the awesome sound! So yes I would definitely recommend this speaker to anybody!

reviews.text: I'm the biggest procrastinator in the world I take a long time making a decision I own several other Bluetooth speakers. This is truly the "crème de la crème" no arguing about it, It's a head turner!

I bought two so I can pair them through NFC and I'm ridiculously impressed. Far more power than Sonos and plenty of wattage to fill any outside environment with your favorite tunes. Well done Aiwa! You've earned a loyal customer!!!

reviews.text: This speaker is amazing its everything you could ever want in a Bluetooth speaker!!!

reviews.title: Baddest Azz Boombox Ever
reviews.text: There you go! Superior full sound!The extended battery pack for an extra $90 lasts forever!
reviews.author: Td77888

reviews.title: Great speaker
reviews.text: The best $300 you will spend on a Bluetooth speaker, awesome bass, crisp highs with absolutely no distortion. Bought it after just watching reviews on YouTube and don't regret it at all. Great Aiwa product as I expected after owning older systems
reviews.author: HUGO69

reviews.title: Cheese cake... sensational
reviews.text:
Finally, a speaker that is worth its asking price. I could not believe how much some of these "portable" bluetooth speakers were going for. I just wanted a speaker that would give me the right punch in terms of loudness without sacrificing sound quality. I demoed all of the expensive bluetooth speakers from various stores and was never impressed.

I stumbled upon the aiwa exos 9 online and was quickly remenisced about this familiar brand as i had various portable cd players and boom boxes from my youth. The amount of positive reviews ive seen from here, amazon and certain forums were convincing for me to try.

I was thoroughly impressed by how quickly the item shipped to me considering how i dont live in the continental u.s. The speaker has a nice size and i love the look and styling of it. It was light, but not at all flimsy or something of inferior quality. I just wish the volume knob was black as well to give it a steath look all around.

And now for the best part: sound. This thing is off the heezy! It was just what i was looking for - loud, tight bass, nice mids and highs. I also love how quickly it is able to sync with my android phone even from afar (30 yards). I even compared it side by side to the beats beatbox that my brother has and my girlfriends bose soundlink 3. I already knew it was superior, but seeing the faces of my brother and girlfriend when they heard my aiwa exos 9 compared to theirs was priceless. Especially when they heard how much i paid for it. Lets just say their sodium levels went up hehe. Awesome job aiwa, and keep up the good work best buy. Yall are killing it right now as dont even bother to shop on amazon when it comes to electronics.
reviews.author: Ruck

reviews.title: Perhaps The Best Portable Boombox Ever!
reviews.text:
First off, I'm 50 years old. I was around way back in the 80's (hehe), when Boomboxes were all the rage, and I've owned most of the top dogs. JVC's M70/90, PC-3, PC-5, and quite a few others I that I can't recall the models. Over the past 20 years I've purchased (and still own) the original KaBoom, RV-DP100/200, and more recently the original Harman Kardon G0-Play (phenomenal), and the Altec Lansing IMT-810 (Very underrated)! My first (and only) BT portable was the Kicker Amphitheater (which is the only one listed that doesn't take batteries).

Not being able to actually physically see, touch & hear the Exos-9, and only going by all of the extremely positive feedback I've been reading on various sites, I decided to take my chance. So I went all in and ordered two!

Well they arrived today and all I can say is I'm elated that I went for it. These things ROCK! I thought the Altec 810 was a beast, and it is, but the Exos 9 is heads and tails above it, and all others I've listed! The highs are crisp, but not overbearing. The mids are where they need to be, and the bass is insane! I highly doubt that any other battery operated portable can reach the volumes this monster does. And without distorting!!

Some people say it's to heavy to be considered a portable. I say fill up your water weights and get real. it's around 15 pounds, it's not that heavy. Try hauling around a JVC M-90 with 10 D-cells for a day, and then get back to me about weight.

Anyways, for anyone who's on the fence about ordering this bad boy, I say get it. or better yet, get two and pair them. You won't regret it !!

And on a side note for those who are looking for a carry case, I just picked up two American Tourister "atmosphera two" luggage carriers at Walmart (item number 62221-1041), and they are a perfect fit. You can even leave the Exos 9 in the carrier while listening to perhaps the best Boombox ever!
reviews.author: MusicMan

reviews.title: Best $300 I have ever spent
reviews.text: This speaker is amazing! The highs are as crisp as burnt toast, and the bass will blow the windows out of your house. Finally, someone has produced a home stereo-quality speaker that is portable - I love that I can bring the party anywhere! Best $300 I have ever spent.
reviews.author: DanaT

reviews.title: Spectacular!
reviews.text: This speaker is spectacular! The volume, base and clarity are things I would expect from a wired speaker, I've owned high-end Bose 901 speakers in the past and currently own large floor model JBL's. The Exos-9 blows them away. I find myself giving demos and raving about this speaker. I'm ordering another to link and pair.
reviews.author: Roger

reviews.title: One word: AWESOME
reviews.text: Hands down the best bluetooth speaker for the money on the market! There is no possible way you will dislike this speaker. Operates at full power unplugged 200 watts! You can link two together with a 50-foot blue tooth range and have true stereo sound. One word: Awesome!!
reviews.author: MTouhy

reviews.title: Superlative quality
reviews.text: The sound quality of the Exos 9 is simply the best I have heard at that price point (or even twice price) . I am surprised, and frankly relieved, they did not price the speaker at the same as the Bose Sound Touch System IV. I can't wait to hear new products from the resurrected Aiwa brand.
reviews.author: Hammer5361

reviews.title: Fantastic Speaker
reviews.text: I was very excited about trying a larger speaker. My tiny bluetooth speakers were great for the office and taking on a business trip, but just couldn't get the job done in a large room.... Let alone outdoors. Aiwa has built the perfect speaker for what I needed. I love the interface and ease of setup but make no mistake, this thing is all about the quality of sound. It is big! It is not ideal for taking on a plane, but has a chargeable battery to move around with ease. Go ahead....embarrass your friends and remind them what music is supposed to sound like! There is nothing else like it. I'm ordering a second one so I can have paired stereo in my backyard.
reviews.author: Sullmeist
